---
title: "Newton & the resistor divider"
subtitle: "T3 · Phase T3.2 · `va-core::newton`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete — ladder rung 1.** The resistor divider was the first circuit this
project ever solved end to end, and (2026-07-17) the first to formally pass against real QSPICE
golden (`t6-integration/03-validation.qmd`).
:::

## Goal

Find the operating point of a nonlinear circuit: iterate `x_{n+1} = x_n − J⁻¹f(x_n)` until the
residual and the update both settle.

## Where it fits

```
 &[&dyn ModelInstance] ──[newton::solve]──► x (the operating point)
                              │
                     loops: mna::assemble → linsolve::solve_dense → update x
```

## The idea

**Convergence needs two conditions, not one.** `solve_from` declares convergence when *either*
the residual's infinity norm drops below `abstol`, *or* every unknown's applied update this
iteration is smaller than `reltol·|x| + abstol` — the second check matters because a circuit can
sit at a residual just above `abstol` indefinitely (a stiff nonlinearity, a badly-scaled unknown)
while genuinely having stopped moving; requiring only the residual check would either loop
needlessly or need an unreasonably loose `abstol`.

**The per-unknown tolerance in that second check isn't always the same number.** `solve` builds
a per-unknown `abstol` table via `mna::classify_abstol` once, up front — any
`ModelInstance::unknown_abstol` override (ultimately sourced from a Verilog-A model's own
`nature...abstol`, § the nature-metadata wiring in `t1-frontend/03-elaboration.qmd`) replaces the
global default just for that unknown. The *residual*-norm gate stays one global scalar, deliberately
— reweighting an `inf_norm` check into a per-row form is a separate design question, not
attempted.

**Junction limiting is a blanket clamp, not a per-device one — a real, stated tradeoff.**
`NewtonConfig::limit_junctions` (on by default) clamps each iteration's proposed update via
`convergence::limit_junction`, using one fixed `vt`/`vcrit` threshold for *every* unknown, not
just genuinely exponential junction voltages — because `va-core` has no way to distinguish a
diode's junction voltage from an ordinary linear node from the ABI alone. This keeps a stiff
exponential device (a diode, a BJT) from overflowing on a cold start, at the cost of possibly
slowing convergence on unknowns that were never exponential to begin with.

## The code

```rust
pub struct NewtonConfig {
    pub max_iters: usize,       // default 100
    pub abstol: f64,            // default 1e-12
    pub reltol: f64,            // default 1e-9
    pub limit_junctions: bool,  // default true
    pub gmin_steps: usize,      // default 0 — see t3-core/03-nonlinear-dc.qmd
}

pub fn solve(
    instances: &[&dyn ModelInstance],
    dim: usize,
    cfg: NewtonConfig,
) -> Result<Vec<f64>, CoreError>
```

Always starts from the zero vector. Each inner iteration:

```rust
let mut sys = mna::assemble(instances, &x, dim);
sys.shunt_gmin(&x, gmin, kinds);                      // no-op unless gmin-stepping is active
let dx = linsolve::solve_dense(&sys.jacobian, &neg_f, dim)?;
for i in 0..dim {
    x[i] = if cfg.limit_junctions {
        convergence::limit_junction(x[i] + dx[i], x[i], vt, vcrit)
    } else {
        x[i] + dx[i]
    };
}
```

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-core newton::
running 7 tests
test newton::tests::solves_resistor_divider ... ok
test newton::tests::solves_diode_resistor_clamp ... ok
test newton::tests::reports_non_convergence ... ok
test newton::tests::per_unknown_abstol_override_changes_the_convergence_decision ... ok
...
test result: ok. 7 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 17 filtered out; finished in 0.23s
```

`solves_resistor_divider` is checked against the closed-form analytic midpoint, not just "the
tool agrees with itself" — for a linear circuit, exact agreement is the bar. This same circuit,
driven through the real netlist pipeline (`t6-integration/{01-netlist,02-cli}.qmd`) and compared
against genuine QSPICE output, is ladder rung 1 — the first to satisfy `CLAUDE.md` §7's actual
definition of "passed":

```console
$ cargo run -q -p xtask -- validate
[xtask]   PASS circuits/divider.net: error=0.000e0 (tol 1e-4)
```

## Limitations

**Always cold-starts from `x=0`** — no `.ic`/initial-guess override for a circuit whose real
operating point is far from zero (the same limitation `t2-codegen/03-charge-and-coverage.qmd`
notes for the charge channel, and the reason QSPICE golden generation needed a `UIC`-based
workaround for `t4-transient`'s transient circuits — see `t6-integration/03-validation.qmd`'s
SPICE-gotcha section).

**Junction limiting is blanket, not per-device** (above) — a real, measured tradeoff, not a bug.

## What's next

`t3-core/03-nonlinear-dc.qmd` — `gmin` stepping and DC sweeps, for circuits plain Newton alone
can't reach.
